intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the Explicit Encoding cleanup to replace platform-dependent encoding with explicit StandardCharsets.UTF_8 constants.

item.title: Understand Platform Encoding Issues
item.description:
Java APIs like new FileReader(file) and String.getBytes() use the platform default charset when no encoding is specified. This causes inconsistent behavior across different operating systems and locales (JEP 400, Java 17+).
The Explicit Encoding cleanup transforms these calls to use java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.UTF_8 (or another specified charset), making the code portable and predictable.
Example transformations:
• new FileReader(f) → new FileReader(f, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
• str.getBytes() → str.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
• Files.readAllLines(path) → Files.readAllLines(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)

item.title: Enable the Cleanup
item.description:
Open the Clean Up dialog to enable the Explicit Encoding cleanup for your project:
1. In the Package Explorer, select a Java project
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select Use custom profile and click Configure…
4. On the Explicit Encoding (Sandbox) tab, enable Add explicit encoding argument
5. Click OK
Click Perform to open the Source menu.

item.title: Select Encoding Behavior Mode
item.description:
The cleanup supports two behavior modes:
• KEEP_BEHAVIOR — only adds the charset argument where the API supports it and where it was previously using the platform default. This preserves existing runtime behavior while making it explicit.
• ENFORCE_UTF8 — always uses StandardCharsets.UTF_8, even if the original code used a different charset. Use this mode when you want to standardize all encoding to UTF-8.
Choose the mode that fits your project requirements in the cleanup profile configuration.

item.title: Run the Cleanup on a Java File
item.description:
Apply the cleanup to one or more Java files:
1. Open or select Java files that use encoding-sensitive APIs (e.g. FileReader, FileWriter, InputStreamReader)
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Confirm the profile has Explicit Encoding (Sandbox) enabled
4. Click Finish
The cleanup will add the charset argument to all affected method calls and constructors.

item.title: Review the Changes
item.description:
After the cleanup runs, inspect the modified code:
• FileReader and FileWriter constructors now include StandardCharsets.UTF_8
• InputStreamReader / OutputStreamWriter constructors now include the charset
• String.getBytes() and new String(bytes) now include the charset
• Files.readAllLines() and similar NIO methods now include the charset
Use Ctrl+Z (Undo) if you want to revert the changes, or open the Git Staging view to review the diff.
